body.title: DickSimnel: max-turns hit should escalate with specific reason, not DONE: gate message
body.result: MAX_TURNS: sentinel in toolloop.run(); _post_result detects it and escalates with clear 'max turns hit' reason; 4 new tests
body.intention: I intend that when DickSimnel hits max_turns, CC picks up the escalated ticket knowing the real reason (ran out of turns) not a misleading one (DONE: missing).
body.description:
**Problem:** When ToolLoop hits max_turns (20), it returns the last assistant content (possibly empty). Then _post_result() sees no DONE: prefix → escalates with reason 'result missing DONE: prefix'. But the real reason is 'max turns hit'. This misleads CC when diagnosing the escalation.

**What to build:** In `_poll_and_work()`, detect max-turns return by adding a sentinel or checking the result against the loop's turn count. Better approach: have ToolLoop return a named result (dataclass or dict with {text, hit_max_turns: bool}) so the caller knows why it terminated.

Simpler fix: ToolLoop.run() returns a string starting with 'MAX_TURNS:' when max_turns is hit (same as DONE: protocol). _post_result() handles MAX_TURNS: by escalating with a clear 'max turns (20) hit' reason.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py, devices/dicksimnel/device.py, tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_toolloop.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Max-turns signaling only
**Completion criteria:** Test shows: ToolLoop hitting max_turns returns text starting with 'MAX_TURNS:'; device escalates with reason containing 'max turns'. 2 new tests.
